channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: About 7 million kids live in a home with a loaded and unlocked gun, a study finds
item.description: The new study in JAMA Network Open also finds that more parents leave their guns loaded and unlocked when they have teenagers, despite the fact that suicide risk goes up for this age group.
item.content:encoded:
The new study in JAMA Network Open also finds that more parents leave their guns loaded and unlocked when they have teenagers, despite the fact that suicide risk goes up for this age group.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: The SPLC survived firebombs and death threats. Will it survive Trump 2.0?
item.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center is facing a serious threat from the Justice Department — and comes at a time when several former and current SPLC employees say the group is already deeply vulnerable.
item.content:encoded:
The Southern Poverty Law Center is facing a serious threat from the Justice Department — and comes at a time when several former and current SPLC employees say the group is already deeply vulnerable.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: FDA commissioner resigns after tumultuous tenure
item.description: The resignation ends Dr. Marty Makary's management of the Food and Drug Administration, which was marked by turmoil and controversy.
item.content:encoded:
The resignation ends Dr. Marty Makary's management of the Food and Drug Administration, which was marked by turmoil and controversy.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: Alabama will split U.S. House primaries, after the Supreme Court's voting ruling
item.description: Alabama will hold a special primary election for four of its seven congressional districts, after the U.S. Supreme Court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map that had been blocked by the courts.
item.content:encoded:
Alabama will hold a special primary election for four of its seven congressional districts, after the U.S. Supreme Court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map that had been blocked by the courts.

(Image credit: Kim Chandler)
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Swasey

item.title: Israel's government is expected to collapse over ultra-Orthodox military draft
item.description: An ultra-Orthodox Jewish party in Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's coalition has called for parliament to be dissolved, threatening to bring down the government.
item.content:encoded:
An ultra-Orthodox Jewish party in Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's coalition has called for parliament to be dissolved, threatening to bring down the government.

(Image credit: Leo Correa)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy filmed a reality show, funded by firms he regulates
item.description: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.
item.content:encoded: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: She spent a year using AI to do almost everything. Here's what she learned
item.description: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.
item.content:encoded: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Inflation jumps to its highest level since 2023. Here are 3 things costing a lot more
item.description: Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.
item.content:encoded:
Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Trump heads to China for state visit. And, how the war in Iran has affected inflation
item.description: Trump leaves for Beijing today for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. And, a new inflation report is expected to show the war in Iran's impact on the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
Trump leaves for Beijing today for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. And, a new inflation report is expected to show the war in Iran's impact on the U.S.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The economic chilling effect of Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.
item.content:encoded:
New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky
